---
title: "Geomorphology"
subtitle: "Study of landforms, their processes, and the forces that shape Earth's surface."
author: "Geography Team"
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
---

Welcome to the **Geomorphology** module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course explores the origin, evolution, and classification of landforms across Earth's surface — from towering mountain ranges to the smallest river meanders.

---

## UGC NET Syllabus

* **Continental Drift, Plate Tectonics, Endogenetic and Exogenetic forces.**
* **Denudation and Weathering, Geomorphic Cycle (Davis and Penck).**
* **Theories and Process of Slope Development.**
* **Earth Movements (seismicity, folding, faulting and vulcanicity).**
* **Landform Occurrence and Causes of Geomorphic Hazards (earthquakes, volcanoes, landslides and avalanche).**

---


{{< include geomorphology/course-content.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/fundamental-concepts.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/endogenic-processes.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/exogenic-processes.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/fluvial-geomorphology.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/glacial-periglacial-landforms.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/coastal-geomorphology.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/aeolian-processes.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/applied-geomorphology.md >}}
